summary: Full backend regression of the modular-router refactor (server.py 4331→68 lines, 10 route modules under /app/backend/routes/). Built /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_regression.py covering all 59 endpoints across auth, iPads/Pool, students, assignments, contracts, imports/exports (incl. NEW 'Vertrag vorhanden'/'Vertrag validiert' columns), settings, admin users, data-protection, and RBAC. Result: 24/25 PASSED, 1 FAILED — one real security regression: /api/data-protection/cleanup-old-data is no longer admin-only.

critical.issue: Missing require_admin(current_user) call. Module imports require_admin but never invokes it, so ANY authenticated standard user can purge data older than 5 years. This is a RBAC regression from the refactor — before refactor the legacy server.py enforced admin role.
critical.fix: Add `require_admin(current_user)` as first line of cleanup_old_data() (same pattern used in admin_users.py:113, 138, 188, 233, 302).

minor.issue: Accepts `status` as a query parameter instead of JSON body. Inconsistent with all other PUT endpoints in the codebase which use Pydantic body models. Frontend / API consumers may pass it in body and silently fail with 422.

Rate-limit is 5/min per IP. When tests run sequentially, repeated logins exhaust the budget within a single test run. Consider adding `Retry-After` header (already standard with slowapi) and documenting the limit in test_credentials.md (added).
require_admin is imported but unused in routes/data_protection.py — Ruff/flake8 would flag this. A lint pass on the routes/ package would catch the security gap above automatically.

action_items: CRITICAL: Add `require_admin(current_user)` to /api/data-protection/cleanup-old-data in routes/data_protection.py line 62 (function body)
Normalize PUT /api/ipads/{ipad_id}/status to accept JSON body (Pydantic model) instead of query parameter
Run `ruff check backend/routes/` to catch unused imports (would have detected the security regression)
Ensure libmagic1 stays installed in pod recovery (already in Dockerfile per iteration_4 notes)
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is structurally clean: server.py is now a 68-line entry-point, 10 route modules + 7 core + 5 model files, single shared api_router with /api prefix. All 59 endpoints respond. Good split.

SECURITY REGRESSION: routes/data_protection.py imports require_admin but never calls it on the only endpoint in the module — silently downgraded from admin-only to authenticated-only. This kind of bug is exactly what static lint (unused import) would catch; recommend adding ruff/flake8 to pre-commit.
PUT /ipads/{id}/status uses a bare query parameter `status: str` while every sibling endpoint uses Pydantic body models. Standardize.

/auth/me cookie auth gap from iteration_4 was NOT in scope this round but appears unchanged — get_current_user still reads only Authorization header (verified by grepping). Page reload with HttpOnly cookie still fails until frontend re-sends Bearer.

Refactor split is shy of guidance for individual modules: routes/imports_exports.py is large (~970 lines, 3 export endpoints with duplicated header/contract-lookup logic). Extract shared 'build_assignment_row(contract_lookup, ...)' helper to DRY-up the 3 export builders.

core/router.py global singleton pattern works but makes import-order matter: server.py must import the route modules AFTER importing api_router or routes vanish. A `routes/__init__.py` could explicitly assemble the modules so server.py imports are explicit.

test_credentials.test_user_pattern: TEST_RF_<6hex>_user with password Pass1234! — created+deleted per session

seed_data_creation: All test artifacts prefixed TEST_RF_<6hex> (iPads, students, users) and cleaned up via session-scope teardown using /admin/users/{id}/complete + /students/batch-delete + DELETE /ipads/{id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 59 endpoints are wired and respond after the refactor — verified via direct CRUD assertions, not just route-existence. NEW Excel columns 'Vertrag vorhanden' and 'Vertrag validiert' confirmed present in all 3 export endpoints: GET /assignments/export (full + filtered), POST /assignments/export-selected, GET /exports/inventory. Pool lifecycle (create+claim+release+bulk-claim+admin-assign-to-user+403-for-non-admin) all green. Rate-limit 5/min is functional. The single critical finding is the missing require_admin call in routes/data_protection.py line 62. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_regression.py is fully reusable; junit XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/refactor_regression.xml. Note: login rate-limit (5/min) is hit when running back-to-back, _login() helper retries with 8s × 20 to cover that.
rca_of_the_issue: Cleanup-old-data RBAC bug: During refactor of monolithic server.py the cleanup_old_data() handler in routes/data_protection.py was moved with its `Depends(get_current_user)` dependency intact, but the inline `if current_user.role != 'admin': raise 403` (or require_admin call) was dropped. The import statement at top of file still pulls in `require_admin`, which is a tell-tale sign the call was meant to be there. Reproduction: login as standard user → POST /api/data-protection/cleanup-old-data → expect 403, got 200. Fix: add `require_admin(current_user)` at top of the function body, identical pattern to admin_users.py.
